stem: The denominator \( (x+[[a]])(x+[[b]])(x+[[c]]) \) has three distinct linear factors. Using partial fractions, evaluate \( \int_{0}^{1} \frac{[[k]]}{(x+[[a]])(x+[[b]])(x+[[c]])}\,dx \).
solution: Decomposing \( \frac{[[k]]}{(x+[[a]])(x+[[b]])(x+[[c]])} = \frac{A}{x+[[a]]} + \frac{B}{x+[[b]]} + \frac{C}{x+[[c]]} \) and integrating gives a sum of three logarithms, evaluating to \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reverses the sign of the combined logarithm expression.
This doubles the correct value.
This halves the correct value.
This scales the correct value by 3/2.
This scales the correct value by 1/3, as if only one of the three log terms were kept.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Each linear factor below has a leading coefficient other than 1. Using partial fractions, evaluate \( \int_{0}^{1} \frac{[[k]]}{([[m]]x+[[a]])([[n]]x+[[b]])}\,dx \).
solution: Factor each denominator as \( [[m]]\left(x+\tfrac{[[a]]}{[[m]]}\right) \) and \( [[n]]\left(x+\tfrac{[[b]]}{[[n]]}\right) \), decompose into partial fractions, and integrate the resulting sum of logarithms to get \( [[answer]] \).

stem: In the partial fraction decomposition \( \dfrac{[[k]]}{(x+[[a]])(x+[[b]])} = \dfrac{A}{x+[[a]]} + \dfrac{B}{x+[[b]]} \), find the constant \( A \) (using the cover-up method: set \( x=-[[a]] \) in \( \dfrac{[[k]]}{x+[[b]]} \)).
